items.title: Tiong calls for urgent talks over diesel price impact on tourism
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing has instructed his ministry to initiate immediate consultations with the Finance Ministry over the impact of rising diesel prices on the tourism transport sector. In a statement, Tiong said his ministry is closely and carefully monitoring the recent increase in diesel prices and understands the challenges faced by operators, particularly the sudden rise in operating costs for those heavily dependent on diesel. Recognising that the tourism transport sector is a key pillar of the national tourism industry, Tiong said while addressing current pressures, the government must also consider the long-term health of this sector.

items.title: Prasarana submits recovery action plan to Transport Ministry to fix Kelana Jaya LRT disruptions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — Prasarana Malaysia Berhad (Prasarana) has submitted a comprehensive recovery action plan to the Transport Ministry following the recent service disruptions on the Kelana Jaya Light Rail Transit (LRT) line. Prasarana Malaysia Berhad Group president and chief executive officer Amir Hamdan said the plan, which focuses on specific action items regarding both the technical aspects of operations and the overall customer experience, was presented during a post-cabinet meeting recently. Amir explained that Prasarana maintains a thorough and standard reporting schedule with APAD whenever an incident occurs to ensure full transparency and compliance with safety standards.

items.title: ‘Unhealthy and inappropriate’: Loke tells Guan Eng, Penang CM to end public spat on quit rent
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke Siew Fook today called for an end to the public dispute between party veteran Lim Guan Eng and Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow over the state’s revised quit rent rates, saying the matter should be resolved internally. In a statement, Loke said the party leadership was “deeply concerned and disturbed” by the ongoing exchanges, and said internal differences over public policy should not be aired in a way that undermines the party’s own state government. Loke said Lim, as a former DAP secretary-general and former Penang chief minister, should appreciate the importance of party discipline.

items.title: Over 20,000 gather at Johor MB’s Raya open house in show of Bangsa Johor unity
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 21 — Over 20,000 people of various ethnicities flocked to Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi’s Hari Raya Aidilfitri open house today, with the extraordinary attendance reflecting the spirit of ‘Bangsa Johor’ and the close relationship between the state government and the people. Private executive Nor Intan Ilyana Rahman, 34, said that organising an open house offers a more meaningful experience as it provides a space for direct interaction between leaders and the people. Programmes like this make the people truly feel appreciated,” she said.

items.title: Iran's new Supreme Leader absent on Persian New Year as speech read out on TV
items.summary: TEHRAN: People in Iran eagerly awaited a first public appearance by the country's new Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, on Persian New Year, or Nowruz, on Friday - but a speech attributed to him was instead read out on state television. Mojtaba Khamenei also lost his wife, mother and a brother-in-law in the bombings. According to the US government, Mojtaba Khamenei is injured.

items.title: Towards a more caring, united and resilient society
items.summary: I would like to extend my warmest wishes of Selamat Hari Raya Aidilfitri, Maaf Zahir and Batin to all Muslims in Malaysia. This year's Aidilfitri is observed in a spirit of reflection, as the world continues to grapple with ongoing conflict in the Middle East, alongside many global challenges that are affecting lives everywhere. TAN SRI LEE LAM THYE National Unity Advocate Kuala Lumpur © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Govt studying targeted support for tourism transport sector after diesel price hike, says Tiong King Sing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing has directed his ministry to begin urgent consultations with the Ministry of Finance following concerns over the impact of rising diesel prices on the tourism transport sector. He said the diesel price increase had placed sudden strain on operators who rely heavily on the fuel, and warned that unmanaged cost escalation could “trigger a chain reaction affecting inbound tourism, businesses and the overall stability of the tourism ecosystem.” Tiong said the government must balance immediate challenges with long‑term sector health, noting the need to strengthen safety standards, vehicle performance and service quality. He said any measures introduced would follow a “stabilise and upgrade” approach to help operators navigate current pressures while encouraging gradual improvements, including vehicle renewal, appropriate financial support and regulatory enhancements.

items.title: King hosts Aidilfitri reception at Istana Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, the King and Queen of Malaysia, today graced the Hari Raya Aidilfitri reception at Istana Negara here. The event was attended by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his wife Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, other cabinet ministers and representatives of foreign diplomatic missions, including those from the United Kingdom, Iran, Azerbaijan and Indonesia. Among the more than 600 guests were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul, Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh, and Malaysian National News Agency chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai and editor-in-chief Arul Rajoo Durar Raj.

items.title: King and Queen host Aidilfitri reception at Istana Negara, attended by rulers, leaders and diplomats
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, the King and Queen of Malaysia, today graced the Hari Raya Aidilfitri reception at Istana Negara here. The event was attended by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his wife Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, other Cabinet ministers and representatives of foreign diplomatic missions, including those from the United Kingdom, Iran, Azerbaijan and Indonesia. Among the more than 600 guests were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul, Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh, and Malaysian National News Agency chairman Datuk Seri Wong Chun Wai and editor-in-chief Arul Rajoo Durar Raj.

items.title: US allows 30-day sale of Iran oil at sea in bid to tame prices
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The Trump administration has issued a 30-day sanctions waiver allowing the sale of Iranian oil currently at sea, a strategic move aimed at cooling global energy prices amid the ongoing conflict with Tehran. The waiver, announced on Friday by the US Treasury Department, permits the purchase of approximately 140 million barrels of Iranian oil. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the move is designed to relieve pressure on energy supplies and "use Iranian barrels against Tehran" to keep prices down during the military campaign, dubbed Operation Epic Fury.

items.title: Muslims across Malaysia welcome Syawal with Aidilfitri prayers
items.summary: In PERAK, the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, performed the Aidilfitri prayers together with about 2,000 congregants at the Ubudiah Mosque in Kuala Kangsar. Muslims gather for Hari Raya Aidilfitri prayers on the first day of Syawal at Masjid Putra in Putrajaya on March 21, 2026 — Picture by Raymond Manuel In KEDAH, the Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, performed the Aidilfitri prayers at that Zahir Mosque in Alor Setar together with more than 2,000 congregants. Muslims perform Aidilfitri sunnah prayers at the Al-Muktafi Billah Shah Mosque, Ladang in Kuala Terengganu March 21, 2026.

items.title: Car plunges into ditch while avoiding dog in Alor Gajah, killing one and injuring five
items.summary: The man died while receiving treatment at Alor Gajah Hospital, according to the New Straits Times. Melaka Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Superintendent Muhammad Zaki Ramat said the car was carrying six occupants, including five Indonesians and a four‑year‑old Malaysian child. He said the 39‑year‑old driver suffered chest and leg injuries, while a 40‑year‑old foreign woman sustained injuries to her right hand and left leg, and the child had minor injuries.

items.title: Aidilfitri tragedy as fire guts three terrace houses in Kota Baru
items.summary: The 8.27am blaze left two units completely gutted, while a third was 80 per cent destroyed. "Upon arrival, the operations team found three double-storey terrace houses on fire. "Two units were 100 per cent destroyed, while another was 80 per cent burnt.

items.title: Expert urges mask‑wearing and self‑care during Aidilfitri to curb TB spread
items.summary: He said that individuals with a cough are at risk of being carriers of the disease, which can infect high-risk groups, including children under five years old, the elderly, and those with chronic diseases such as diabetes and cancer who have weak immune systems. Dr. Nurazam said the disease caused by the mycobacterium tuberculosis bacteria, which attacks the lungs, can spread and infect other individuals through the air when the active tuberculosis carrier talks, coughs, sneezes, shouts or sings. “Open house events held in open areas have a low risk of this disease spreading due to good ventilation compared to closed halls and houses, but it should be noted that they pose a high risk to chronic disease sufferers whose immune systems are weak in fighting the disease,” he said.

items.title: One colour, one heart: 52 family members coordinate for Raya
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: For the past eight years, the family of Muda Abdullah and Khatijah Said has turned heads every Hari Raya with their tradition of wearing matching festive attire. Muda, 68, and Khatijah, 62, view the coordinated outfits as more than just a style choice; it is a symbol of the togetherness they have built within their large family. Since 2018, the family has rotated through various themes, including emerald green, navy blue, purple, sage green, soft grey, maroon, royal blue and this year's choice, brick orange.

items.title: Haiyaa: Uncle Roger to 'slim down' operations after announcing outlet closure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Restaurant chain "Fuiyoh! It's Uncle Roger" says it will be "slimming down" its operations after announcing the closure of its branch at the IPC Shopping Centre. In 2024, Malaysian comedian Nigel Ng, better known as "Uncle Roger" announced he would open his first restaurant in Malaysia.

items.title: Iran fires missiles at joint US-UK base in Indian Ocean
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Iran recently fired two ballistic missiles at the joint US-UK military base on Diego Garcia, suggesting Tehran possesses a longer strike range than previously estimated. Iran fired two ballistic missiles toward the joint US-UK military base at Diego Garcia in the Indian Ocean, according to a report by The Wall Street Journal on Friday citing US officials. Diego Garcia, located in the Chagos Islands, is one of two bases Britain allows the United States to use for operations regarding Iran.

items.title: Kampung Batu Enam skies light up in 40-year Raya eve tradition
items.summary: "The fireworks display in this village has become a major attraction on the eve of Aidilfitri. Visitor Muhammad Ismadi Alias, 38, said the village's reputation for its fireworks tradition is well-known. "Various types of fireworks are set off here, drawing large crowds.

items.title: NST Leader : Making balik kampung safer
items.summary: THE great Malaysian balik kampung ritual to celebrate Hari Raya Aidilfitri is also an uncomfortable paradox. Traffic police statistics compiled from their Ops Selamat operations between 2022 and 2025 reaffirms: about 31,000 accidents, 461 of them fatal, causing 495 deaths, including 211 motorcyclists. Total accidents dropped from 12,407 to 9,859 in 2025.

items.title: Iconic Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad ready for Raya family photos, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh has invited Muslims celebrating Hari Raya Aidilfitri today to visit the newly revitalised Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad for family photos. "If you have time, stop by Bangunan Sultan Abdul Samad in Kuala Lumpur with your family. Block One of the Sultan Abdul Samad Building Complex opened on Feb 2, marking the first completed phase of Khazanah Nasional Bhd's Dana Warisan restoration initiative.

items.title: Sultan Mizan calls for Aidilfitri to serve as a bridge to strengthen unity
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, March 21 — The Sultan of Terengganu, Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin, called for Aidilfitri to be used as a bridge to strengthen unity and harmony in the lives of the multiracial community in the state. However, as Muslims celebrate the joy of the occasion, Sultan Mizan also said that those who are fortunate and able should remain mindful and show compassion towards those who are less fortunate His Royal Highness also called on the people to take the opportunity to multiply their good deeds while also giving the poor the chance to experience the joy of Aidilfitri. Sultan Mizan also advised the people to celebrate Aidilfitri in moderation, and to visit one another in order to enliven and strengthen the bonds of kinship.

items.title: Sabah-based journalists find new meaning in Raya away from home
items.summary: "This year I decided to celebrate Raya here because I wanted to experience the Hari Raya atmosphere in Sabah. I try to recreate that by connecting online with family, preparing simple festive dishes and decorating my home," he said, describing Sabah's celebrations as relaxed and culturally diverse. He said he tried to create the festive atmosphere by connecting online with his family, preparing simple festive dishes and decorating his home.

items.title: Pulau Burung landfill in Penang hit by second fire in two months, containment efforts underway
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL, March 21 — The Pulau Burung landfill here caught fire again early this morning, the second blaze in two months. Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations John Sagun Francis said they received a call at 5.40 am and dispatched fire engines from the Nibong Tebal and Sungai Bakap fire stations to the scene. He added that the fire is still not under control, with operations ongoing involving 18 personnel, including supervisors from both stations, assisted by volunteer fire brigades from Sungai Jawi, Nibong Tebal, Valdor and Parit Buntar.

items.title: Round‑the‑clock Customs needed at Pengerang Port as it faces eight‑to‑10‑hour clearance delays, says maritime expert
items.summary: The senior maritime executive with over 30 years of industry experience said the customs office currently serving Pengerang Port is located in Sungai Rengit, approximately 20 minutes away, creating challenges for time-sensitive operations, particularly outside normal working hours. “Customs operations are limited to standard office hours, from 8 am to 6 pm, and are closed on weekends, while port activities operate 24 hours a day. Documentation processes at Pengerang Port can take between eight and 10 hours, compared with less than three hours at the Port of Singapore,” he said.

items.title: Ten killed, four missing in South Korea car plant fire
items.summary: DAEJEON: Ten people were killed and four remain missing after a massive fire broke out at a car parts plant in Daejeon on Friday. Firefighters are currently using rescue dogs and tearing down internal structures to locate the four missing people. "We plan to deploy rescue workers and continue the search for those missing," an official said.

items.title: Guan Eng, Chow in fresh spat over Penang quit rent hikes
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The open exchanges between Bagan member of parliament Lim Guan Eng and Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow over Penang's quit rent hikes show no signs of abating. Lim has intensified his criticism, claiming that many voters would not have supported the state leadership if they had known the 2023 state election manifesto allowed for such steep increases in quit rent rates. Lim said this includes significant quit tax hikes deemed necessary for economic stability.

items.title: Langkawi ferry services to cut to three trips daily after Aidilfitri as diesel prices jump over 100pc
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 21 — The ferry service schedule to Langkawi will be revised after the Aidilfitri holidays due to rising industrial diesel prices by over 100 per cent. Ferry Line Ventures Sdn Bhd general manager, Dr Baharin Baharom, said the restructuring is needed to reduce the risk of losses faced by Langkawi ferry operators as operational costs increase. “At present, ferry operators in Langkawi charge a fare of 88 sen per nautical mile, which is significantly lower than the rates charged by operators for other major islands, such as Pulau Pangkor and Pulau Tioman, which reach RM2 per nautical mile.

items.title: One killed, five injured after car veers off road while avoiding dog
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: A 43-year-old foreigner died, and five others were injured when their car veered off the road while swerving to avoid a dog at Jalan Garing, Machap Baru, yesterday. The deceased, a man, died while receiving treatment at Alor Gajah Hospital following the 4.15pm accident. "The 39-year-old driver sustained chest and leg injuries, while a 40-year-old foreign woman suffered injuries to her right hand and left leg.

items.title: King, Queen perform Aidilfitri prayers at Istana Negara [WATCH]
items.summary: In attendance were Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, alongside Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and his wife, Datin Seri Hamidah Khamis. Also present were Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, and Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan. The congregation included Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Dr Johari Abdul, Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh and several foreign representatives.

items.title: US sending Marines and amphibious assault ship to Middle East, officials say
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US military is deploying 2,500 Marines and an amphibious assault ship to the Middle East, officials said on Friday, as President Donald Trump accused NATO allies of "cowardice" for not assisting in reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The narrow waterway, which carries roughly 20% of global oil and liquefied natural gas, has been closed to most traffic since the US and Israel launched a war against Iran nearly three weeks ago. To mitigate a global economic shock, the Trump administration said it would waive sanctions to allow the sale of 140 million barrels of Iranian oil currently stranded on tankers.

items.title: King and Queen perform Aidilfitri prayers at Istana Negara, joined by Anwar and Cabinet members
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim and his wife, Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and his wife Datin Seri Hamidah Khamis were also in attendance. Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, (front, right) and Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail (front, 2nd right) perform Aidilfitri Sunat Prayer at the Main Surau of the Istana Negara March 21, 2026. — Bernama pic Also present were Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, and Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan.

items.title: Thousands gather at Masjid Al Bukhary for Aidilfitri prayers
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The spirit of Aidilfitri filled the air in Penang as nearly 5,000 Muslims gathered at Masjid Al Bukhary here this morning to perform the Aidilfitri prayers in a vibrant and meaningful celebration. This year's prayers were held at Masjid Al Bukhary for the first time as the Penang State Mosque is currently undergoing renovation. Across the state, Aidilfitri was celebrated in a spirit of unity and togetherness, reflecting Penang's multicultural harmony.

items.title: US jury finds Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: A federal jury in California found on Friday that tech tycoon Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders, causing a share price plunge as he prepared to buy the company for US$44 billion. After a three-week trial involving in-person testimony from Musk, the jury found that two tweets posted in May 2022 contained false statements responsible for the share price drop. Jurors agreed Musk violated a securities rule barring false and misleading statements that sink a stock price.

items.title: US jury finds Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO: A federal jury in California found on Friday that tech tycoon Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders, causing a share price plunge as he prepared to buy the company for US$44 billion. After a three-week trial involving in-person testimony from Musk, the jury found that two tweets posted in May 2022 contained false statements responsible for the share price drop. Jurors agreed Musk violated a securities rule barring false and misleading statements that sink a stock price.

items.title: King: Elements that threaten national unity must be eradicated without compromise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has decreed that any elements that threaten the unity of the people must be eradicated without compromise. In his Aidilfitri 2026 message shared today via the Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, His Majesty reiterated the advice he had given in last year’s address, which now appears to have been forgotten or ignored by the public. “It is not merely a matter of legal action or discussions among community leaders when concerns and warnings arise, but also the practice of civility in daily life among the people — it must be nurtured and constantly reinforced from time to time,” said Sultan Ibrahim.

items.title: Duty before celebration for RTD enforcement officer this Raya
items.summary: NILAI: For Road Transport Department (RTD) "eye-in-the-sky" enforcement officer Al-Amin Fadhil Mustafa Kamal, Hari Raya Aidilfitri is defined not by celebration at home, but by duty. The Kuala Lumpur-born enforcement officer has spent the past decade serving with the Negri Sembilan RTD, including every festive period on the ground as part of operations to keep traffic in check. Raised in a family of uniformed personnel, Fadhil said working during Raya comes naturally, shaped by a shared understanding of service.

items.title: Man with ‘Ship Floss’ tattoo found dead in Tasik Biru, Kuang; police seeking information
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — The body of a man was found floating in Tasik Biru, Kuang near here on Thursday morning. Sungai Buloh police chief Supt Mohd Hafiz Muhammad Nor said they received a report from the public regarding the discovery at 8.03 am, adding that the victim is about 170 centimetres tall, fair-skinned, and has a tattoo reading ‘Ship Floss’ on his left shoulder. The man was found wearing a dark brown T-shirt with the word ‘Suppligae’ on it, blue jeans, and a Seiko wristwatch with a green strap.

items.title: Family of three dies after motorcycle skids off road on Pulau Tioman
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 21 — A family holiday to celebrate the upcoming Aidilfitri on Pulau Tioman ended in tragedy yesterday when a couple and their young son were killed after the motorcycle they were riding skidded off the road along Jalan Tekek-Juara. Rompin district police chief Supt Sharif Shai Sharif Mondoi the victims were Mohd Sabri Ja’afar, 42, a customer service officer at the National Water Research Institute of Malaysia, his wife Siti Aisyah Suhaime, 32, and their five-year-old son, Muhammad Yusuf Abdurrahman. Preliminary investigations revealed that the victims were riding a Yamaha Ego Avantiz motorcycle from the Kampung Juara waterfall to a resort when the incident occurred at about 4 pm.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim: Eradicate elements threatening national harmony without compromise [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has issued a stern warning that any element threatening national unity must be "eradicated without compromise," calling on the silent majority to speak out against the insolent. In his Aidilfitri message today, His Majesty expressed disappointment that previous reminders on social harmony appeared to have been forgotten. "I wish to reiterate the advice given in last year's Hari Raya message, which many seem to have forgotten or ignored — that any element threatening the unity of the people must be eradicated without compromise," the King said.

items.title: Son of Astro Awani senior editor killed in Tanah Merah crash involving JPJ patrol vehicle
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 21 — The son of an Astro Awani senior editor died in a road accident involving a Road Transport Department (JPJ) patrol vehicle at KM85.5 of Jalan Kota Bharu-Machang-Jeli near Kampung Rahmat, Bukit Bunga, in Tanah Merah yesterday evening. The victim, identified as Mursyid Azmi, 25, son of Husna Yusop, was driving a Perodua Myvi when the collision occurred. Meanwhile, JPJ senior enforcement director Datuk Muhammad Kifli Ma Hassan said the accident, which occurred at about 4.30 pm, involved the victim’s vehicle believed to have skidded into the opposite lane before colliding with a Toyota Fortuner driven by a JPJ officer.

items.title: Week-long clean-up for oil-contaminated Pantai Batu Burok
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The Kuala Terengganu City Council (MBKT) expects to take a week to clear a three-kilometre stretch of Pantai Batu Burok contaminated by black oil waste. Mayor Lizan Che Mat said 40 personnel from the Environmental Health Department and cleaning contractors have been deployed to conduct phased clean-up works. Also present was MBKT Environmental Health Department director Mohd Saiful Bahari Ramli.

items.title: Anwar welcomes Pakistan–Afghanistan ceasefire, urges lasting peace
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has welcomed the temporary ceasefire between Pakistan and Afghanistan in conjunction with the Aidilfitri celebration and urged all parties to continue de-escalation efforts towards lasting peace. Pakistan said it had temporarily suspended military operations in Afghanistan at the request of Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Turkiye in conjunction with Aidilfitri. Pakistan Information Minister Attaullah Tarar said in a statement that the temporary ceasefire began at midnight on Wednesday (March 18) and would last until midnight on Monday (March 23).

items.title: Firefighters battle blaze at Pulau Burung landfill
items.summary: NIBONG TEBAL: A fire broke out at the solid waste disposal site in Pulau Burung here early this morning, with firefighting operations still ongoing. Fire engines from the Nibong Tebal and Sungai Bakap fire stations were dispatched to the scene, arriving at 5.55am. Penang Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director John Sagun Francis said efforts to contain and extinguish the fire are ongoing.

items.title: Malaysia, Pakistan renew commitment to West Asia de‑escalation, says PM
items.summary: NEW DELHI, March 21 — Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and his Malaysian counterpart Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim yesterday agreed to continue efforts to promote peace in West Asia amid the US-Israeli strikes on Iran. “We discussed the evolving situation in Iran and the Gulf region, and agreed on the urgent need for de-escalation and continued joint efforts to promote peace through dialogue and diplomacy,” Shehbaz said in a post on the social media platform X. We discussed the evolving situation in Iran and the Gulf region, and agreed on the urgent need for de-escalation… — Shehbaz Sharif (@CMShehbaz) March 20, 2026

items.title: Pentagon to adopt Palantir AI as core US military system
items.summary: NEW YORK: Palantir's Maven artificial intelligence system will become an official program of record, ensuring the long-term use of its weapons-targeting technology across the US military. Maven is a command-and-control software platform that analyses battlefield data to identify targets. Feinberg said designating Maven as a program of record will streamline its adoption across all military branches and provide stable, long-term funding.

items.title: Anwar: Celebrate Aidilfitri with moderation and compassion, mindful of others’ suffering (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged the people to celebrate Aidilfitri in moderation and to be more mindful of the plight of those who are suffering, as the world faces geopolitical turmoil, especially in West Asia. https://www.facebook.com/BeritaRTM/videos/live-perutusan-aidilfitri-20261447h-yab-perdana-menteri/1630890024622743/ Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said that with proper preparation and planning, the country remains optimistic about the economic position it has achieved, especially in recent years. “And we, the Madani Government, will strive to defend and strengthen this position as challenges mount,” he said.

items.title: 'We have won': Trump signals end to Iran hostilities but rules out truce
items.summary: TEHRAN: President Donald Trump said on Friday that the United States was considering "winding down" military operations against Iran but ruled out a ceasefire with the Islamic republic. The US president said hostilities could soon end as objectives are close to being met, but he rejected a truce while "literally obliterating the other side". Trump added that other nations will have to take responsibility for policing the Strait of Hormuz shipping lane, though he said the United States would assist if asked.

items.title: Despite the distance, Bangladeshi workers find the comfort of ‘home’ in KL for Hari Raya Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 21 — Celebrating Hari Raya away from home is never easy, but when you are thousands of kilometres away on a mission to provide for your loved ones, it's essential to recreate a sense of “home” to keep you going. “I came here when I was just 18 years old, and I remember that I used to cry, especially during the first three years of celebrating Hari Raya here because I missed my family,” said Salemusa, who works at a restaurant in KL. Salemusa said his colleagues would often comfort him during those early years.

items.title: DPMs Zahid, Fadillah highlight kinship, unity and prudence in Aidilfitri messages (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has called on Muslims to continue fostering love, strengthening bonds of kinship and extending sincere forgiveness to one another in conjunction with Syawal. He also prayed that the Syawal celebrations would bring blessings, peace and happiness to all Muslims. https://www.facebook.com/zahidhamidi.fanpage/videos/saya-ingin-mengucapkan-selamat-hari-raya-aidilfitri-maaf-zahir-dan-batin-kepada-/2303819680028279/ Meanwhile, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof urged Muslims to celebrate Aidilfitri this year in a modest yet meaningful way.

items.title: Two killed, four injured after car skids and hits tree in Putrajaya
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 20 — Two men were killed while four others were seriously injured in an accident that occurred in Precinct 1 heading towards Taman Wetland, Precinct 12, here this afternoon. Putrajaya Fire and Rescue Department operations assistant director PgB M Haziq Hazmi said his department received an emergency call via NG999 at 11.58 am before a fire engine from Precinct 14 Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) with an EMRS team rushed to the scene. “A total of six victims were involved in the incident with two men confirmed dead at the scene by the Ministry of Health,” he said via a Facebook post by the Federal Territory of Putrajaya Fire and Rescue Service today.

items.title: State leaders call for stronger kinship and spirit of togetherness this Aidilfitri (VIDEO)
items.summary: Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said the festive day offers all Muslims a chance to deepen relationships, seek forgiveness, and show appreciation for friends, families and the surrounding community. May this Aidilfitri bring happiness, peace, and blessings to all,” he said in his Aidilfitri greeting in a Facebook post today. https://www.facebook.com/dsaminuddinharun/videos/selamat-hari-raya-aidilfitri-maaf-zahir-dan-batin-kepada-seluruh-rakyat-negeri-s/1275481581346349/ Pahang Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail called on the people to make Aidilfitri a moment to strengthen family and community ties and to rekindle the spirit of love among the people.

items.title: Lights, drawings and handmade touches: Anwar ‘truly touched’, thanks students for creative Aidilfitri cards (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has expressed his appreciation to students and Malaysians for the creative Hari Raya Aidilfitri greeting cards sent to him in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidilfitri tomorrow. “Alhamdulillah, I am truly touched to receive various colourful Hari Raya cards showcasing the creativity of children, some with drawings, some even with lights, truly creative! Salam Aidilfitri to everyone,” he said.

items.title: ‘Strengthen unity this Raya’: Ministers urge Malaysians to uphold unity, kindness and mutual respect in Aidilfitri greetings (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 20 — Cabinet ministers urged Malaysians to use Aidilfitri celebrations as ways to strengthen unity, encourage kindness and solidarity, as well as enhance humanitarian values in the country’s multicultural society. Meanwhile, National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang urged Malaysians to forgive one another and to foster closer ties regardless of race, religion and culture, pointing out that the country could preserve harmony and ensure continued peace and prosperity through understanding and mutual respect. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R Ramanan meanwhile invited Malaysians to continue to strengthen their unity, instill kindness and to step forward in the spirit of togetherness towards a brighter future.

items.title: Lorry overturns, causing oil spill on Pan Borneo Highway near Bukit Song; no injuries reported
items.summary: MIRI, March 20 — No injuries were reported following a road incident involving an oil spill after a cargo lorry overturned while descending Bukit Song along the Pan Borneo Highway here on Thursday evening. The Fire and Rescue Department Malaysia (Bomba) Lopeng said it received a distress call at 5.37pm and dispatched a team from the Lopeng fire station to the scene, located about 30km away. Upon arrival at 6.07pm, the operation commander PBK I Rorudi Umas reported that the oil spill was believed to have been caused by a self-accident involving a cargo lorry.

items.title: As global fuel prices climb, how does Malaysia compare with other countries?
items.summary: RON95 now costs RM3.27 per litre and RON97 is RM4.55 per litre, up from RM2.59 and RM3.15 per litre respectively at the end of February. Diesel in Peninsular Malaysia has climbed from RM3.04 to RM4.72 per litre over the same period, while diesel in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan remains at RM2.15 per litre. Regular fuel in the US is priced at RM4.05 (US$1.02) per litre, while the UK’s E10 petrol stands at RM7.66 (1.45 pounds) per litre.

items.title: Resolve minor disputes early to protect unity, stability and national security, says PM Anwar
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, March 20 — Any minor disputes in the country must be addressed promptly to prevent them from escalating into crises that could undermine peace, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He stressed that unity among the people, as well as national stability and security, must remain top priorities as Malaysia faces economic pressures and uncertainties stemming from international conflicts. “Safeguard national peace, especially as we are a country of diverse races and religions.

items.title: Raya rush eases: Traffic smooth on major highways this evening with only slight congestion near Seremban R&R
items.summary: A spokesman for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said that closed-circuit television (CCTV) monitoring indicated steady vehicle movement on northbound, southbound and East Coast-bound routes. “Slight congestion was only detected in a small section near the Seremban Rest and Service (R&R) area heading towards Bandar Ainsdale; however, this has not impacted the overall traffic flow,” he said when contacted by Bernama today. In view of the clear roads, LLM issued a stern warning to motorists against speeding or taking advantage of the light traffic to violate traffic laws, citing an increased risk of road accidents.
